name: Wipro Limited

overview.businessModel: IT Services, Consulting and Business Process Services - Focus on integrated capabilities across cloud, data, AI and cybersecurity

valuation.dcfMethod: 5-year FCF projection with 5% terminal growth, 11.5% WACC

cfoVsPat.observation: CFO higher than PAT at 121%. Good cash generation quality despite margin pressure.

receivablesGrowth.observation: Receivables growing faster than sales - DSO increased to 78 days from 72. Working capital deterioration is a concern.

promoterPledge.observation: Zero pledge by Azim Premji family. Strong governance signal.

relatedPartyTransactions.observation: RPTs at ₹1,567 Cr (1.79% of revenue). Slightly elevated but disclosed. Primarily with IT product division.

contingentLiabilities.observation: Higher contingent liabilities at 3.7% of revenue. Some ongoing tax disputes. Monitor closely.

effectiveTaxRate.observation: Lower effective tax rate (20.6%) due to tax benefits from SEZ units. Transparent.

managementTrackRecord.comments: Struggling transformation under CEO Thierry Delaporte (since 2020). Multiple restructuring efforts but margins have collapsed from 21% to 13%. High leadership churn - 15+ senior exits in 3 years. Strategy unclear - pivoted multiple times. Capital allocation concerns with expensive M&A (Capco at high valuation).

brandStrength.comments: Established brand with 75+ year history. Known for Azim Premji's philanthropic leadership. However, brand perception weakening due to poor financial performance. Client perception surveys show declining satisfaction scores.

innovationCapability.comments: Innovation investments made (Designit acquisition, Wipro Digital) but ROI unclear. Lower R&D spend than peers at 0.6% of revenue. Innovation labs established but limited IP generation. Playing catch-up on AI/GenAI.

customerLoyalty.comments: Customer retention declining to 89% from 94% three years ago. Lost several large clients. Wallet share shrinking in existing accounts. Only 28 $100M+ clients vs 59 for TCS. Cross-selling challenges evident.

employeeCulture.comments: High attrition of 23% (vs industry 15%). Employee morale low due to margin pressure and cost cuts. Multiple restructuring rounds creating uncertainty. Training programs cut back. Glassdoor ratings declined to 3.2/5.

capitalAllocationDiscipline.comments: Questionable capital allocation - Capco acquisition at $1.45B (premium valuation) not delivering expected synergies. Dividend payout maintained at 68% despite EPS decline. ROE collapsed from 24% to 14.5%. ROCE declining trend. Should focus on core rather than expensive M&A.

esgCompliance.comments: Azim Premji Foundation's philanthropic work provides strong ESG credentials. Renewable energy usage at 55%. Good diversity metrics - 35% women. However, recent layoffs impacting social score. MSCI ESG Rating: A.

sustainabilityInitiatives.comments: Carbon neutrality target by 2040 (later than peers). Sustainability initiatives exist but less aggressive than TCS/Infosys. Some green buildings but not comprehensive.

competitiveAdvantage.comments: Weakening competitive position - lost differentiation vs peers. No clear moat visible - margins below industry, growth lagging, client retention declining. Brand and Azim Premji reputation are residual advantages but fading. Needs strategic reset to rebuild moat.

industryRisk.description: Same industry headwinds as TCS/Infosys but less resilient due to weaker competitive position.
industryRisk.mitigation: Attempting to reposition but execution has been poor.

financialRisk.description: Low debt (D/E 0.03). Strong cash position ₹21,234 Cr net cash. No refinancing risk.

governanceRisk.description: Azim Premji's reputation ensures strong governance culture. No major controversies. Transparent disclosures.

regulatoryRisk.description: Similar H1B visa and data regulation risks as peers. NYSE listing adds compliance complexity.

operationalRisk.description: Severe execution challenges - margin collapse, client losses, high attrition (23%), leadership churn. Transformation not delivering results. Project delivery issues emerging.
operationalRisk.mitigation: Multiple restructuring attempts but success uncertain. New leadership needed.

currencyRisk.description: 83% revenue from exports. Lower natural hedge than Infosys. Currency headwinds impacting margins.
currencyRisk.mitigation: Hedging program in place but impact visible in P&L.

competitiveRisk.description: Losing market share to TCS, Infosys, and mid-tier players. Margin collapse from 21% to 13% indicates severe competitive pressure. Client defections. Differentiation lost.
competitiveRisk.mitigation: Turnaround plan announced but execution track record poor. May need 2-3 years to stabilize.

technologyDisruptionRisk.description: GenAI disruption risk amplified by weak competitive position. Less investment in AI compared to peers. Risk of being left behind.
technologyDisruptionRisk.mitigation: Announced AI investments but credibility low given past execution.

geopoliticalRisk.description: Standard geopolitical risks for IT services. US concentration at 56%.

recommendation.investmentThesis: Wipro represents a value trap rather than a value opportunity. Despite trading at a discount to peers (28x PE vs sector 28.5x), this is justified by significantly inferior fundamentals: (1) Margin collapse from 21% to 13% EBITDA - worst among large-cap IT, (2) ROE halved from 24% to 14.5%, (3) Revenue growth anaemic at 3.6% vs sector 8%, (4) Market share loss evident - client retention 89% vs peer 94%+, (5) High attrition 23% impacting delivery, (6) Leadership instability with 15+ senior exits. The transformation under CEO Thierry Delaporte has failed to deliver - multiple strategy pivots, restructuring, and expensive M&A (Capco) have not arrested margin decline. Investment case rests entirely on turnaround hope, but execution credibility is low. Even at current depressed valuations, downside risk outweighs upside. Key concern: Can margins recover or will structural issues (competitive intensity, GenAI disruption) keep them depressed? Our assessment: Structural headwinds dominant. Margin recovery to even 16-17% (vs prior 21%) will take 3+ years. Recommend AVOID until clear evidence of turnaround emerges.
keyCatalysts: Margin stabilization at 14-15% preventing further deterioration
Large deal wins showing competitive traction
Attrition declining below 18% indicating morale improvement
Management commentary providing clarity on strategy
Cost optimization showing results - subcontracting costs down
Client retention improving - stopping the bleeding
keyRisks: Further margin compression to 10-11% if pricing pressure continues
More client losses in BFSI segment
Failed M&A integration - Capco synergies not materializing
Attrition spiral - more exits leading to delivery issues
EPS decline continuing - could fall to ₹12-14 in bear case
recommendation.valuationSummary: Wipro trades at 27.6x FY24 EPS of ₹16.11, in line with sector (28.5x) but this is NOT cheap given quality gap. DCF with conservative assumptions (5% terminal growth, 11.5% WACC) yields ₹385 fair value - 13% below current price. Relative PE valuation at sector average ignores quality discount - should trade at 24-25x given lower ROE, margins, growth. Our target: ₹410 (25.5x FY25E EPS of ₹16) implies 8% downside. Bear case (margin pressure continues): ₹345 (22x on ₹15.5 EPS). Bull case (successful turnaround): ₹480. Risk-reward unfavorable.
recommendation.riskRewardRatio: Unfavorable - 8% downside vs limited upside even in best case. Better opportunities exist.

classificationReasoning: Wipro was a strong #3 player but is now clearly struggling: (1) Margins collapsed 40% from 21% to 13%, (2) ROE halved from 24% to 14.5%, (3) Market share declining, (4) Client retention poor at 89%, (5) High attrition 23%, (6) Multiple failed turnaround attempts. Was good but facing structural challenges. Could recover but needs 2-3 years of flawless execution.

comparisonVsPeers.vsTCS: Significantly behind on all metrics - 0.36x revenue, margins 13% vs 26%, ROE 14.5% vs 37%. Quality gap massive. Trades at discount but still not attractive.
comparisonVsPeers.vsInfosys: Lagging badly - 0.56x revenue, margins 13% vs 22%, slower growth (3.6% vs 9%). Infosys clearly superior. Valuation discount not enough to compensate.
comparisonVsPeers.vsHCL: Similar scale (0.68x HCL) but inferior margins (13% vs 19%), lower ROE (14.5% vs 21%). HCL executing better despite different business mix.
comparisonVsPeers.vsTechM: Comparable revenue (1.05x Tech M) but worse profitability. Both facing challenges but Tech M's telecom exposure at least provides differentiation. Wipro lacks clear positioning.
